title: Merge Bases, Topology, and Integration Strategies · Graph Lens: Gita Merge Base Multiple
summary: Two release lines contain criss-cross merges, a reverted integration, rename-heavy work, and policy requiring explainable ancestry; the integrator must choose and defend topology. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita merge base multiple increment in merge topology and strategy decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.
Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Merge Base Multiple
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G95 is a theory in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G95 is a worked example in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy. A defensible solution applies Gita Merge Base Multiple, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to merge topology and strategy decision record.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor
git merge-base --all release topic
# expected: all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed
# observation: two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration
# changed-case: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy
# transfer-limit: validate resulting behavior in an authorized disposable clone because graph evidence is not semantic proof
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Merge Base Multiple evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-merge-base-multiple-12b57o" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Merge Base Multiple into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-merge-base-multiple-12b57o
# scenario-key: trace-12b57o probe-o75b21 boundary-b57o12 owner-12b57ox fault-12b57oz
# before-ref: main=a11ce12 candidate=b00b17o
# hypothesis: criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor
git merge-base --all release topic
# expected: all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed
# observation: two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration
# changed-case: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base
# after-ref: main=a11ce12 candidate=c0ffeeo
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy
# transfer-limit: validate resulting behavior in an authorized disposable clone because graph evidence is not semantic proof
